category_title: Nonverbal students with ASD using iPads for communication and academic tasks
student_description: This is a nonverbal student with ASD who struggles with independent iPad use, frequently engaging in repetitive stereotypical movements (rapid tapping/swiping), premature screen advancing, and navigating away from AAC apps to play games. The evidence reveals they uniquely require direct physical intervention (hand-over-hand prompting), active professional presence, and strict separation of devices (one for AAC, one for games) to successfully fulfill app functions.

criteria.rationale: Verbal instructions are insufficient to stop this student's repetitive tapping or premature screen advancing; they require direct physical intervention.
criteria.check: The tutor's response includes or recommends direct physical guidance (e.g., hand-over-hand prompting) to help the student complete the action or to physically prevent them from advancing the screen prematurely.

criteria.rationale: When tasked with using an AAC app, this student will quickly navigate away to games if the tutor acts only as a passive observer.
criteria.check: In AAC contexts, the tutor actively engages in a back-and-forth communication exchange with the student rather than simply issuing a verbal command to use the device.

criteria.rationale: The student frequently engages in stereotypical swiping or incorrect device orientation and needs to see the functional action demonstrated.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly models or demonstrates the correct physical interaction with the app (e.g., showing how to move an object rather than just tapping it) before expecting the student to perform it.
